title: OpenClaw Tool Reference
description: Each tools/<tool-id>.json file in an agent workspace conforms to this schema. See spec/openclaw-v1/003-tool-contract.md.

id.description: Kebab-case unique id within this agent.

description.description: One-line summary, used in the architect's tool listings.

tool_kind.description: Either a built-in kind (workspace-read, workspace-write, sandbox-exec, research, plan-validate) or a custom kebab-case id registered with the runtime.

config.description: Tool-specific configuration. Validated against the tool's own schema by the runtime.

stages.description: Lifecycle stages where this tool may be invoked. Omit or null = all stages.

modes.description: Execution modes where this tool may be invoked. Omit or null = all modes.

destructive.description: True if this tool can cause irreversible changes (delete files, send messages, deploy).

concurrency_safe.description: True if multiple invocations may run in parallel. Tools with side effects on shared state are not concurrency-safe.

requires_approval.description: If true, every invocation pauses for human approval. Defaults to whatever the destructive flag implies, unless explicitly set.

ref.description: Key into the credential store. The runtime resolves this to actual secrets at execution time.

schema_ref.description: Path to JSON Schema describing the resolved credential shape.
